database.title: Database Incident Playbook
steps: Check active connections: SELECT count(*) FROM pg_stat_activity;
Review max_connections vs pool size in app config
Check replication lag on replicas if applicable
Increase max_connections or reduce pool size temporarily
Restart connection pool manager after config change

deployment.title: Deployment Incident Playbook
steps: kubectl describe pod <pod-name> for events
Check image tag exists in registry
Verify env vars and secrets in deployment manifest
kubectl rollout undo deployment/<name> if bad release
Redeploy with corrected image tag or config

memory.title: Memory Incident Playbook
steps: kubectl top pod to confirm RSS usage
Restart pod for immediate relief
Check for memory leaks in recent deploys
Review container limits in deployment spec
Apply patch version if known leak exists

api.title: API Integration Playbook
steps: Check third-party API status page
Implement exponential backoff on client
Add request queue with rate cap
Batch operations to off-peak hours
Enable circuit breaker if sustained failures

infrastructure.title: Infrastructure Playbook
steps: Check certificate expiry: openssl s_client or certbot certificates
Renew cert: certbot renew --force-renewal
Restart reverse proxy after renewal
Verify DNS resolution for affected endpoints
Add auto-renewal cron to prevent recurrence
